commit.message:
Add kfake-based Kafka IAM fixture integration tests (#5813)

## Summary
- add a new `kafka_iam_mock` fixture for integration tests
- add a small Go helper (`test/fixtures/tools/kafka_iam_mock`) built on
franz-go `kfake`
- emulate server-side `SASL/OAUTHBEARER` and validate decoded AWS IAM
token fields
- add new IAM test suites for both `from_kafka` and `to_kafka`

## Details
- helper exposes `/health`, `/seed`, and `/auth_events` endpoints for
fixture orchestration and teardown assertions
- fixture builds and runs the helper at startup, seeds deterministic
test messages, and verifies successful auth events before teardown
- tests use `security.protocol=SASL_PLAINTEXT` with `aws_region` +
`aws_iam` options and disable metrics push for deterministic output

## Tests

commit.message:
Extend slice function to support list types

The slice function now accepts both string and list arguments, enabling
element-level slicing on lists with the same begin, end, and stride
semantics as strings. Lists support negative stride for reverse iteration,
while strings do not. Bounds are normalized consistently: negative indices
count from the end, and out-of-bounds indices are clamped to valid ranges.

Added comprehensive integration tests covering basic slicing, begin-only,
end-only, negative indices, stride, negative stride, empty lists, and
direct function calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Document parallel suite execution feature (#222)

Add documentation for the new `mode: parallel` suite configuration option,
allowing tests to run concurrently while sharing fixture lifecycles. Updates
both the write-tests guide and the test-framework reference with examples and
behavior details.

commit.message:
Ensure .sh files default to shell runner (#30)

* Ensure .sh files default to shell runner

Shell test files now always use the "shell" runner by default, even when a
directory-level test.yaml specifies a different runner (e.g., "tenzir").
Explicit runner declarations in test file frontmatter still take precedence.

This ensures that shell scripts work reliably in mixed-runner directories
without requiring redundant frontmatter.

* Add changelog entry for shell runner fix

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Support concurrent execution of suite members (#29)

* Add concurrent suite execution support

Implement parallel execution mode for suite members, allowing tests within a
suite to run concurrently instead of strictly sequentially. The mode is
configured via `suite.mode: parallel` in directory-level test.yaml files.

Key additions:
- SuiteExecutionMode enum supporting sequential and parallel modes
- SuiteConfig dataclass with mode field and normalization functions
- Worker methods for parallel suite execution using thread pools
- Thread-safe fixture depth tracking and run-skipped counters
- Serialization of fixture assertion hooks during parallel execution
- Support for context variable propagation to worker threads
- Comprehensive test coverage for concurrent behavior
- Example parallel suite demonstrating publish/subscribe workflows

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Handle interrupts in parallel suite scheduling

* Add changelog entry for parallel suite execution

Co-Authored-By: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

* Cap parallel suite workers by --jobs

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

commit.message:
Add read_tql operator (#5707)

Resolves TNZ-231

## Summary
- Implement `read_tql` parser for deserializing TQL-formatted records
from bytes input
- Support all TQL data types: strings, numbers, booleans, null, lists,
nested records
- Add 15 integration tests covering basic types, native Tenzir types,
string escaping, nested structures, and whitespace handling

## Test plan
- [x] Wait for `.stdin` support in test framework to land
- [x] Run integration tests: `TENZIR_BINARY=... uvx tenzir-test --root
test tests/operators/read_tql`
- [x] Verify all 15 tests pass

commit.message:
Add operator requirements to GCS and ABS integration tests (#5820)

## Summary
- bump `tenzir-test` in `nix/package.nix` to `v1.7.0`
- add `requires.operators` to cloud operator top-level suites:
  - `from_gcs` requires `from_gcs`
  - `from_abs` requires `from_azure_blob_storage`
- opt the affected suites into capability-based skipping via `skip.on:
capability-unavailable`
- keep lifecycle sub-suites inheriting the top-level requirements (no
duplicated `requires` blocks)

## Validation
- `PATH="$PWD/build/xcode/release/bin:$PATH"
/Users/mavam/.local/bin/tenzir-test --root test -m "operators/from_gcs"
-m "operators/from_abs" --summary -j 1`
  - result: 10 skipped, 0 failed (missing operators are now skipped)

<sub>resolves TNZ-345</sub>

commit.message:
Fix code review findings for read_tql and slice operators

- Add recursion depth limits to prevent stack overflow (SEC-1, SEC-2)
- Eliminate quadratic buffer operations with offset tracking (PRF-1)
- Extract common buffer processing logic (PRF-3, ARC-2)
- Add string reserve optimization in json_string_parser (PRF-2)
- Add docs URL to ReversePlugin (ARC-3)
- Add boundary tests for stride=0, empty reverse, single reverse

commit.message:
Add read_tql operator and integration tests

Implement `read_tql` parser for deserializing TQL-formatted records from
bytes input. The parser supports all TQL data types including strings,
numbers, booleans, null, lists, and nested records.

Integration tests cover basic types, native Tenzir types (IP, subnet,
duration, time, blob), string escaping, nested structures, whitespace
handling, and edge cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Avoid Python backtraces on Ctrl+C interrupts (#31)

* Avoid Python backtraces on Ctrl+C interrupts

Interrupting test runs with Ctrl+C now exits cleanly without leaking
Python tracebacks from subprocess or fixture startup/teardown paths.
Interrupt-shaped errors (including nested causes with signal-style
return codes such as 130) are treated as graceful cancellation so
the CLI reports interrupted tests instead of crashing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add PR number to changelog entry

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Avoid Python backtraces on Ctrl+C interrupts (#31)

* Avoid Python backtraces on Ctrl+C interrupts

Interrupting test runs with Ctrl+C now exits cleanly without leaking
Python tracebacks from subprocess or fixture startup/teardown paths.
Interrupt-shaped errors (including nested causes with signal-style
return codes such as 130) are treated as graceful cancellation so
the CLI reports interrupted tests instead of crashing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add PR number to changelog entry

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix non-eventable stdin fallback (#5815)

## Summary

- Refactor `from_stdin` operator to cleanly separate eventable and
blocking read backends with explicit `BackendResult` return types
- Extract `ProducerSink` helper to centralize chunk queue management and
ensure the terminal sentinel is enqueued exactly once
- Add `TENZIR_FROM_STDIN_BACKEND` environment variable override for
debugging backend selection
- Run blocking reads on the CPU executor to avoid blocking the IO thread
- Add integration tests for backpressure with `tail` and unterminated
EOF handling

## Test plan

- [x] Added `backpressure_tail` integration test
- [x] Added `unterminated_eof` integration test
- [x] CI passes

commit.message:
Improve parallel suite scheduling and correctness (#33)

* Improve parallel suite scheduling and correctness

Add min_jobs field to parallel suites so they can declare the minimum
job count required for correct execution. Validate this upfront and fail
fast if underprovisioned. Prioritize parallel suite execution over
standalone tests to ensure interdependent test groups start together.
Warn when parallel suites have more tests than available jobs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix O(n^2) queue pops when suites are exhausted

* Share Worker default locks across shared resources

* Enforce min_jobs on reserved parallel suite workers

* Keep suite priority until reservation completes

* Prioritize pending parallel suites over sequential suites

* Share default suite queue state across workers

* Wait for required suite slots before min_jobs failure

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Allow intro-only releases for re-publishing after yanking (#9)

* Allow intro-only releases for re-publishing after yanking

Releases can now include only intro text without changelog entries.
This enables quick re-publishes after pulling a package artifact.
Previously, creating a release without entries would fail. Now it
only fails if there are neither entries nor intro text.

Changes:
- Removed early guard rejecting releases with no entries
- Moved entry table rendering to conditional display
- Added validation requiring entries XOR intro text
- Updated docstring to reflect new capability
- Added tests for intro-only via --patch, explicit version, and
  rejection of truly empty releases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add changelog entry for intro-only releases feature

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Switch changelog author from claude to codex

* Format tests/test_cli.py
